description_long: This is a futuristic, angular airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a predominantly olive green and black color scheme. The wings are sharply swept back and mounted low on the fuselage, with integrated engine nacelles positioned near the rear of the wings. The cockpit is forward-mounted and enclosed with a dark, tinted canopy, suggesting a single or dual-seat configuration. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from the rear. The surface appears to be made of metal or composite materials, with visible panel lines and mechanical details, giving it a high-tech, tactical appearance. No visible landing gear is present, implying it may be retractable or designed for a specialized landing system.
description: A sleek, angular olive green and black airplane with swept low wings, rear-mounted engines, and a forward-enclosed cockpit. The design is futuristic and tactical, with a conventional tail and detailed surface features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, low-mounted swept wings, and rear engine nacelles.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed nose, wide wing span, and forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the engine nacelles and wing structure.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and engine exhausts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and no visible winglets; wings are integrated with engine nacelles and exhibit a slight anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear of the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from the rear fuselage.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear; likely retractable or internal.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a dark canopy, likely single or dual-seat.
